completed_topics.topic: Section 453 Installment Note Dispositions and PTET Credit Recapture Rules for Real Estate Partnerships 2026
completed_topics.description: Tax consequences when partnerships sell or dispose of installment notes before full payment after electing PTET: whether PTET credits claimed in year of sale must be recaptured or amended when note is disposed in Year 3-5, impact on partner-level credit carryforwards when accelerated gain recognition occurs due to note disposition, coordination with Section 453B gain/loss on note disposition (fair market value vs. remaining basis), state-specific PTET amendment procedures when installment obligations are satisfied early (CA, NY, NJ, MD procedures), interaction with Section 453(d) election to recognize gain in year of sale after PTET was already paid, documentation requirements for tracking PTET credit adjustments when note dispositions trigger gain acceleration, modeling combined tax impact when partnerships monetize installment notes through third-party buyers while PTET credits remain in carryforward status for partners, and audit defense strategies when PTET credit utilization timelines diverge from federal installment reporting due to mid-stream note dispositions

completed_topics.topic: Section 453 Installment Sale Reporting and PTET Election Timing Mismatches for Real Estate Partnerships 2026
completed_topics.description: How PTET elections interact with Section 453 installment sale reporting for real estate partnerships: whether PTET payments are due in year of sale or year of installment receipt, coordinating PTET elections when partnerships report gain ratably over multiple years, impact on partner K-1 reporting when PTET is paid upfront but installment income is recognized over 5-10+ years, state-specific PTET timing rules for installment sales (CA vs. NY vs. NJ approaches to prepaid PTET on deferred gain), interaction with Section 453(l) interest charge on large installment obligations when PTET creates basis disparities, documentation requirements for tracking PTET credits across installment periods, coordinating PTET revocations if partnerships dispose of installment notes before full payment, and modeling combined tax impact when PTET elections affect both year-of-sale entity-level tax and partner-level installment income recognition for partnerships selling appreciated real estate with seller financing

completed_topics.topic: Section 1031 Like-Kind Exchange Basis Carryover and PTET Election Coordination for Real Estate Partnerships 2026
completed_topics.description: How PTET elections affect Section 1031 like-kind exchange basis calculations for real estate partnerships: whether PTET basis adjustments carry over to replacement property, coordinating PTET elections across multiple exchange cycles, impact on deferred recapture potential when PTET reduces relinquished property basis before exchange, state-specific PTET treatment for multi-state 1031 exchanges (different PTET election statuses in relinquished vs. replacement property states), timing mismatches when PTET is paid in year of relinquished property transfer but replacement property is acquired in subsequent year, documentation requirements for PTET basis tracking through exchange cycles, interaction with boot allocation when PTET creates disparities between federal and state basis calculations, and modeling combined tax impact when PTET elections affect both deferred gain calculations and replacement property depreciation schedules for partnerships executing multi-property 1031 exchanges

completed_topics.topic: Real Estate Partnership PTET Basis Adjustment Tracking and Depreciation Recapture Coordination 2026
completed_topics.description: How PTET elections interact with depreciation recapture (Section 1250/1245) and basis adjustment tracking in real estate partnerships: whether PTET payments reduce depreciable basis before or after recapture calculations, coordinating PTET with cost segregation studies when partnerships have accelerated depreciation, impact on unrecaptured Section 1250 gain calculations when PTET reduces partnership inside basis, state-specific recapture treatment for PTET-electing real estate partnerships (CA vs. NY vs. NJ approaches), timing mismatches when PTET is paid in year 1 but property sales with recapture occur in year 2+, documentation requirements for dual tracking when PTET creates disparities between federal and state depreciation schedules, interaction with like-kind exchanges (Section 1031) when PTET-electing partnerships defer gain, and modeling combined tax impact when PTET elections affect both recapture ordinary income and capital gain characterization for partnerships holding commercial or residential real estate with significant accumulated depreciation

completed_topics.topic: Section 734(b) and 743(b) Basis Adjustments with PTET Elections: Coordination Rules 2026
completed_topics.description: How PTET elections affect optional and mandatory basis adjustments under IRC Sections 734(b) and 743(b): whether PTET payments reduce inside basis before or after basis adjustments on distributions and transfers, coordinating PTET with Section 754 elections when partnerships have multiple built-in gain layers, timing mismatches when PTET is paid in year 1 but distributions/transfers occur in year 2+, impact on transferee partners who purchase interests in PTET-electing partnerships mid-year, state-specific basis adjustment rules for PTET partnerships (CA vs. NY vs. NJ treatment of PTET in basis calculations), documentation requirements for dual tracking when PTET creates disparities between inside basis (Section 734(b)) and outside basis (Section 743(b)), and modeling combined tax impact when PTET elections interact with basis adjustments for partnerships holding appreciated real estate or other Section 704(c) property with layered allocation methodologies

completed_topics.topic: Section 704(c) Remedial Allocation Methods and PTET Book-Tax Disparity Tracking 2026
completed_topics.description: How PTET elections affect remedial allocation methods under Section 704(c) regulations: whether PTET payments create new book-tax disparities requiring remedial allocations, coordinating PTET with ceiling rule limitations when partnerships hold contributed property with built-in gains, impact on reverse Section 704(c) allocations when PTET reduces book capital accounts differently than tax capital accounts, state-specific book-tax disparity treatment for PTET electing partnerships, documentation requirements for remedial allocation tracking when PTET creates disparities between book and tax depreciation/amortization, and modeling combined tax impact when PTET elections interact with Section 704(c) layered basis tracking for partnerships holding appreciated real estate or other contributed property

completed_topics.topic: Section 754 Election Timing and PTET Basis Adjustment Coordination 2026
completed_topics.description: Strategic timing of Section 754 elections relative to PTET elections and partnership distributions: whether to make Section 754 elections before or after PTET payments, impact on transferee partners purchasing interests in PTET-electing partnerships, coordinating Section 743(b) and Section 734(b) adjustments when PTET reduces outside basis, state-specific Section 754 recognition rules for PTET partnerships, documentation requirements for dual elections (PTET + Section 754), and modeling combined tax impact when both elections affect basis tracking for partnerships holding appreciated real estate or other Section 704(c) property

completed_topics.topic: IRC Section 734(b) Basis Adjustments and PTET Distribution Interactions 2026
completed_topics.description: How PTET elections affect optional basis adjustments under IRC Section 734(b) when partnerships make distributions: whether PTET payments reduce the amount of basis adjustment available on distributions, coordination with Section 754 elections when partnerships hold appreciated assets, timing mismatches when PTET is paid in one year but distributions occur in subsequent years, impact on transferee partners who purchase partnership interests mid-year, state-specific basis adjustment rules for PTET electing partnerships (CA vs. NY vs. NJ approaches), and documentation requirements for Section 734(b) tracking when PTET creates disparities between inside basis and outside basis in partnerships with significant unrealized appreciation

completed_topics.topic: Reverse Section 704(c) Allocations and PTET Revaluation Events 2026
completed_topics.description: How PTET elections affect partnership revaluations under Section 704(b) regulations and reverse Section 704(c) allocations: whether PTET payments trigger revaluation events, coordinating PTET with book-ups on partner admissions/withdrawals, impact on built-in gain tracking when partnerships hold appreciated assets, interaction with disguised sale rules when PTET allocations shift economic burdens, state-specific revaluation requirements for PTET electing partnerships, and documentation standards for reverse Section 704(c) tracking when PTET creates new book-tax disparities

completed_topics.topic: Section 704(c) Property Contributions and PTET Allocation Methodologies 2026
completed_topics.description: How PTET elections interact with Section 704(c) property contributions (traditional, remedial, and curative allocation methods): whether PTET benefits should be allocated proportionally among all partners or specially allocated to partners who bear the state tax burden, impact on book-tax disparities when PTET reduces federal taxable income but not book income, coordinating PTET with reverse Section 704(c) allocations on revaluation events, documentation requirements for special PTET allocations in partnership agreements, and audit defense strategies when PTET allocations diverge from standard profit-sharing ratios in partnerships holding appreciated real estate or other Section 704(c) property

completed_topics.topic: Multi-Tier Partnership PTET Basis Adjustment Timing and IRC Section 705 Coordination 2026
completed_topics.description: When tiered partnerships elect PTET at multiple levels, tracking partner basis adjustments under IRC Section 705(a) and 705(b): whether PTET payments by lower-tier partnerships increase upper-tier partnership's basis in lower-tier interest (as deemed contributions) or decrease basis (as distributions), timing mismatches when lower-tier PTET is paid in one tax year but upper-tier K-1 is issued in subsequent year, interaction with Section 704(c) reverse Section 704(c) allocations in tiered structures, coordinating basis adjustments when PTET credits are claimed by ultimate partners vs. carried forward at entity level, state-specific basis treatment for tiered PTET (CA vs. NY vs. NJ approaches), and documenting basis tracking methodologies for audit defense when partnerships use different accounting methods (cash vs. accrual) across tiers

completed_topics.topic: Tiered Partnership PTET Allocation and Credit Cascading Rules 2026
completed_topics.description: PTET treatment for tiered partnership structures (upper-tier partnerships owning interests in lower-tier partnerships): which entity level makes PTET election, cascading credit allocation through multiple tiers, avoiding double taxation when both tiers elect PTET, state-specific rules for tiered PTET treatment (NY, CA, NJ variations), Section 704(b) capital account tracking through tiers, and coordinating PTET elections when upper-tier and lower-tier partnerships have different partner compositions and state nexus profiles

completed_topics.topic: PTET Basis Adjustments and Partner Capital Account Tracking 2026
completed_topics.description: How PTET payments affect partner basis calculations under IRC Section 705: whether PTET payments increase partner basis (as deemed contributions) or decrease basis (as distributions), timing of basis adjustments (payment date vs. K-1 issuance), interaction with Section 704(c) built-in gains, capital account maintenance under Section 704(b) regulations, state-specific basis treatment variations, and coordinating basis adjustments when PTET credits are claimed vs. carried forward vs. refunded

completed_topics.topic: PTET Credit Allocation and K-1 Reporting for Multi-Owner Coaching Practices 2026
completed_topics.description: Best practices for allocating PTET credits among multiple practice owners on Schedule K-1: proportional vs. special allocation methods, state-specific K-1 reporting requirements (CA Schedule K-1 568, NY IT-204-IP, NJ Schedule NJ-K-1), coordinating PTET credits when owners have different resident states, documentation requirements for owner-level tax preparers, and handling PTET credit mismatches when owners file in non-PTET states

completed_topics.topic: Multi-State PTET Election Optimization for Coaching Practices with Variable Income 2026
completed_topics.description: Dynamic PTET election strategies for coaching practices with fluctuating income: modeling optimal election thresholds based on income volatility, coordinating PTET elections with quarterly estimated tax payments, handling PTET true-ups when actual income diverges from projections, state-specific amendment procedures for increasing/decreasing PTET elections mid-year, and risk management for practices that over-elect PTET relative to final tax liability

completed_topics.topic: PTET Unused Credit Carryforward and Refund Coordination Across States 2026
completed_topics.description: Handling excess PTET credits when entity-level tax payments exceed owner-level tax liability: state-by-state carryforward rules (some states allow 5-year carryforward, others no carryforward), refund procedures for departing owners, coordinating carryforwards when owners reside in different states than the entity, interaction with composite filing credits, and documentation requirements for tracking unused PTET credits across multiple tax years

completed_topics.topic: PTET Payment Acceleration and Year-End Planning Strategies 2026
completed_topics.description: Timing PTET payments to optimize tax benefits across years: accelerating payments into current year vs deferring to next year, interaction with estimated tax safe harbors, coordinating PTET payments with owner-level cash flow needs, state-specific payment timing rules (quarterly vs annual), and strategies for practices with variable income to maximize PTET deduction value while avoiding cash flow crunches

completed_topics.topic: PTET Revocation and Amendment Procedures Across States 2026
completed_topics.description: State-by-state procedures for revoking or amending PTET elections after initial filing: revocation deadlines (vary by state), amended return requirements, handling overpayments and refunds, coordinating revocations across multiple states when business circumstances change, and IRS guidance on PTET election reversals for audit defense

completed_topics.topic: SALT Cap Workaround Layering: PTET + DAF + CRT Coordination Strategies 2026
completed_topics.description: Advanced multi-layer SALT cap avoidance for high-income coaching practices: sequencing PTET elections with DAF contributions and charitable remainder trust funding, optimizing combined federal deduction impact, state-by-state analysis of stacked workaround effectiveness, avoiding AMT triggers when layering multiple strategies, and documentation requirements for audit defense when using coordinated SALT avoidance structures

completed_topics.topic: PTET Payment Timing and Estimated Tax Coordination for Multi-State Coaching Practices 2026
completed_topics.description: Strategic timing of PTET elections and payments to optimize estimated tax obligations: quarterly vs annual payment options by state, safe harbor rule interactions, avoiding underpayment penalties when PTET credits reduce resident state tax due, coordinating PTET payments with federal estimated tax calculations (Form 1040-ES), and year-end PTET acceleration strategies for high-income years

completed_topics.topic: DAF Grant Distribution Timing and Multi-State Tax Apportionment for Coaching Practices 2026
completed_topics.description: How DAF contributions and grant distributions interact with multi-state income apportionment: which states allow DAF contribution deductions, timing strategies for maximizing state-level tax benefits, coordinating DAF grants with nexus thresholds, and whether DAF grant recommendations create charitable activity nexus in recipient states for coaching practices with multi-state operations

completed_topics.topic: PTET Refundable Tax Credit Coordination Across Resident and Non-Resident State Filings 2026
completed_topics.description: How multi-state coaching practices claim PTET credits on resident state returns: credit calculation methodologies (dollar-for-dollar vs. tax-due limitation), avoiding double taxation when multiple states have PTET elections, resident state conformity variations (some states don't recognize PTET credits), Schedule K-1 credit allocation among owners, and documentation requirements for audit defense when claiming cross-state PTET credits

completed_topics.topic: QBI Deduction Optimization and PTET Coordination for Multi-State Coaching Practices 2026
completed_topics.description: Modeling the interaction between Section 199A QBI deductions and PTET elections: how entity-level tax payments reduce K-1 income and QBI base, threshold planning for SSTB phase-ins, coordinating PTET with W-2 wage and UBIA limitations, and determining optimal PTET payment levels that maximize combined QBI + SALT savings for coaching practices operating across multiple states

completed_topics.topic: Donor-Advised Fund Cryptocurrency Contribution Strategies 2026
completed_topics.description: Maximizing tax benefits when contributing appreciated cryptocurrency to donor-advised funds (DAFs): fair market valuation requirements, avoiding private inurement issues, DAF provider comparison (Fidelity Charitable, Schwab, Vanguard, crypto-native options), timing strategies for volatile assets, and integrating DAF giving with CRT exit planning for comprehensive philanthropic tax optimization

completed_topics.topic: Pass-Through Entity Tax (PTET) Elections and SALT Cap Workarounds for Multi-State Coaching Practices 2026
completed_topics.description: State-level PTET elections as federal SALT deduction workaround: which states offer PTETs (CA, NY, NJ, MA, CT, etc.), election deadlines and revocation procedures, benefit vs cost analysis for coaching practices, coordinating PTETs across multiple states, and impact on shareholder-level tax credits and composite filing strategies

completed_topics.topic: Cryptocurrency Trust Charitable Remainder Trust (CRT) Conversion Strategies 2026
completed_topics.description: Using charitable remainder trusts to defer or eliminate capital gains tax on appreciated cryptocurrency holdings: CRT setup requirements, payout rate optimization (5-7% standard), tax-free asset sale inside CRT, income stream characterization to beneficiaries, remainder charity selection, and comparing CRTs vs IDGT installment sale strategies for digital asset portfolios exceeding $500,000 in unrealized gains

completed_topics.description: Advanced planning to minimize state-level franchise taxes and minimum fees across jurisdictions: California $800 minimum + 1.5% net income tax, New York fixed dollar minimums based on NY source income, composite filing cost-benefit analysis, entity structuring (LLC vs S-Corp vs C-Corp) by state, and strategies for coaches operating in high-franchise-tax states while maintaining multi-state compliance

completed_topics.description: Strategic timing of crypto trust distributions to beneficiaries across tax years: coordinating with beneficiary income levels to minimize aggregate tax burden, leveraging 0% long-term capital gains brackets, avoiding trust compressed tax rates, and sequencing distributions of high-basis vs low-basis assets for optimal after-tax outcomes

completed_topics.description: When to terminate portions of crypto trusts vs full termination: decanting assets to new trusts in favorable jurisdictions, partial termination thresholds under state law, retaining vs distributing appreciated assets, and sequencing distributions to minimize aggregate tax burden across multiple beneficiaries

completed_topics.description: Advanced strategies for S-Corp owners serving clients across state lines: withholding rate optimization, avoiding double withholding, reciprocal agreement utilization, state unemployment insurance (SUI) rate management, and quarterly reconciliation processes for multi-state payroll compliance

completed_topics.description: When and how to terminate crypto trusts: partial vs full termination, in-kind distribution of digital assets to beneficiaries, basis implications, wallet transfer protocols, and coordinating distributions across multiple jurisdictions with varying tax treatments

completed_topics.description: When coaches should elect S-Corp status based on multi-state income patterns: reasonable compensation benchmarks by state, payroll tax savings vs compliance complexity, state-level S-Corp recognition rules, and integrated tax planning for coaches exceeding $150K net income

completed_topics.description: Moving existing crypto trusts to more favorable jurisdictions, decanting outdated trust terms to new instruments, trustee removal and replacement procedures, and tax consequences of trust migration for digital asset holders

completed_topics.description: Safe harbor rules, penalty avoidance strategies, and state-by-state estimated tax payment requirements for coaches with income sourced to multiple jurisdictions: Form 1040-ES optimization, state voucher systems, and timing strategies for variable coaching income

completed_topics.description: How coaches can guide clients through digital asset inventories, legacy contact setup, and integrating video/audio legacy tools with traditional estate planning documents

pending_topics.topic: Section 453B Disposition Gain Character and PTET Allocation Methodologies for Real Estate Partnerships 2026
pending_topics.description: Character of gain recognized under Section 453B when installment notes are disposed (ordinary income vs. capital gain vs. Section 1250 recapture) and how PTET allocations should be characterized on partner K-1s: whether PTET credits retain character from original sale (capital gain from real estate) or follow Section 453B gain character (potentially ordinary income if note is dealer property), impact on partner-level net investment income tax (NIIT) calculations when PTET credits are claimed against ordinary income vs. capital gain, state-specific character rules for PTET (CA vs. NY vs. NJ treatment of PTET on ordinary vs. capital income), coordinating PTET allocations when partnerships have mixed-character gains from note dispositions, documentation requirements for character tracking on Schedule K-1 (Box 4 vs. Box 9 coding), interaction with Section 751(a) hot assets when partnerships dispose of installment notes received for depreciable real estate, and modeling combined tax impact when PTET credit character diverges from federal gain character due to Section 453B disposition rules

description: How PTET elections interact with Section 453 installment sale reporting for real estate partnerships: whether PTET payments are due in year of sale or year of installment receipt, coordinating PTET elections when partnerships report gain ratably over multiple years, impact on partner K-1 reporting when PTET is paid upfront but installment income is recognized over 5-10+ years, state-specific PTET timing rules for installment sales (CA vs. NY vs. NJ approaches to prepaid PTET on deferred gain), interaction with Section 453(l) interest charge on large installment obligations when PTET creates basis disparities, documentation requirements for tracking PTET credits across installment periods, coordinating PTET revocations if partnerships dispose of installment notes before full payment, and modeling combined tax impact when PTET elections affect both year-of-sale entity-level tax and partner-level installment income recognition for partnerships selling appreciated real estate with seller financing
